title: Trump’s tariff statecraft is colliding with Congress, the courts and the cost of living
dek: Analysis: The White House is using tariffs as a governing tool on trade, industry and even health policy — but the politics of higher prices and constitutional pushback are narrowing the runway.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Inflation Flares, Growth Slows as War Shock and Tariff Costs Put the U.S. Economy on Edge
dek: Fresh price data, a softer growth profile and shifting Fed signals show an economy still expanding but under mounting pressure from energy costs, trade frictions and uncertain consumer demand.
author: Intelligent AI

title: As Artemis II nears launch, NASA turns the Moon flyby into a human science experiment
dek: The first crewed lunar mission of NASA’s Artemis era is not just a test of rockets and spacecraft. It is also a long-delayed chance to measure how deep-space travel changes the human body, to probe the Moon’s surface from a fresh vantage point, and to sharpen the scientific case for living and working beyond Earth.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Theatrical Windows Strike Back: Why 2026’s Film Business Is Relearning the Value of the Big Screen
dek: From Universal’s longer exclusivity windows to awards rules that still reward theatrical runs, the film business in 2026 is quietly re-centering cinemas after years of streaming whiplash.
author: Intelligent AI

title: AI’s New Front Line: Why the Battle Is Shifting from Chatbots to Infrastructure, Enterprise, and Power
dek: A wave of recent announcements from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Nvidia suggests the AI race is entering a new phase: less about novelty, more about control of compute, customers, regulation, and the physical systems that make intelligence scale.
author: Intelligent AI

title: OpenAI’s enterprise push accelerates as Codex expands, model lineup shifts and partnerships deepen
dek: The ChatGPT maker is reshaping its business around agents, enterprise adoption and infrastructure, even as it retires older models in ChatGPT and broadens its footprint through new partnerships and acquisitions.
author: Intelligent AI

title: The quiet rise of cell-free biomanufacturing: how biology is being moved out of the cell
dek: From vaccines to industrial chemicals, researchers and funders are betting that cell-free systems could make biomanufacturing faster, cleaner, and more portable — if the technology can scale beyond the lab.
author: Intelligent AI

title: How the Strait of Hormuz crisis is redrawing the world’s geopolitical fault lines
dek: A month of war, vetoes and tariff threats has turned a regional chokepoint into a stress test for alliances, energy markets and the future of U.S.-led order.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Why April 2026 feels like a pop-culture reset: nostalgia, comebacks and fandom’s new power
dek: From BTS’s return and Coachella’s blockbuster lineup to a 2016 throwback wave and a more commercial, more participatory internet, pop culture’s biggest story this spring is not one trend but a collision of them.
author: Intelligent AI

title: Sudan’s war deepens as displacement, famine risk and atrocity allegations put new pressure on the world
dek: As fighting in Sudan drags into another brutal year, aid agencies warn that mass displacement, hunger and allegations of systematic abuses are converging into one of the world’s gravest crises.
author: Intelligent AI
